body.title: Reply-forms-Pursuit: wrap Igor reply action in Pursuit to prevent progress loss
body.result: Wrapped _process_network_msg reply dispatch in Pursuit (parent/child pattern via existing primitives). 8 new tests. Commit 76a3b117. Gated IGOR_PURSUITS_ENABLED.
body.description:
Fix the 'reply eats further progress' bug by wrapping Igor's reply action in a Pursuit.

Bug shape: When Igor replies to a message, the reply action consumes the current goal/attention slot without creating a 'I am committed to this response and what comes after' trace. Without that commitment trace, there's no continuation goal, and attention drops after the reply — Igor stops rather than continuing to pursue the upstream work.

This shares mechanism with the BOREDOM/foreman-scan bug: action fires without goal-adoption (commitment moment + anticipatory trace + continuation Pursuit spawn).

Pursuit shape for reply:
1. Reply stimulus arrives (message, question, observation requiring response)
2. 'reply-to-Akien' Pursuit formed: goal-facia, anticipatory dopamine trace
3. Reply action fires (existing machinery)
4. Completion test: was reply sent?
5. Dopamine ding on sent
6. Sub-Pursuit spawn: if upstream work remains, spawn continuation Pursuit
   (The critical step — without this, nothing follows the reply)

Affected files: verify before sprinting — likely cognition/ reply path or brainstem/ (flag HIGH-inertia if so). Size may expand from S to M depending on T-single-pursuit-test-case findings (goal_formation machinery may need to exist first).

Gate: T-single-pursuit-test-case — need the Pursuit pattern proven and tooling available before wiring reply.

Test plan (minute-cadence protocol):
- Send Igor a message that has obvious follow-on work
- At minute 1: did Igor reply AND spawn a continuation goal?
- At minute 2: is the continuation goal being pursued, or did attention drop?
- Contrast pre/post: check cognition.log for goal-facia formation event around reply sends
